title: Improvement proposals (FR-6.3)
description: The cheap-model synthesis output: concrete, path-contained diffs against versioned assets. Each proposal targets a SINGLE allowlisted asset (prompts/, pipelines/, schemas/, policy.yaml) and carries the literal unified diff plus a rationale. The engine re-validates path-containment (review F-001) and `git apply --check`s every diff before any human is asked to approve it (FR-6.4); a proposal never self-applies.

slug.description: Short kebab-case identifier for the proposal, e.g. `triage-rubric-false-bikeshedding`.

target_path.description: Repo-relative path of the single versioned asset this diff edits. Must fall inside the allowlist (prompts/, pipelines/, schemas/, policy.yaml); anything else is rejected at parse time (review F-001).

rationale.description: Why this change helps, traced to the feedback / retrospective evidence that motivated it.

diff.description: The literal unified diff in `git apply` format (---/+++/@@ hunks), repo-relative paths, touching only `target_path`.
